text: The surgeon assembles the surgical setup by inserting the beta dip into a 15 mm port.
The surgeon uses scissors to stretch the rectus sheath, visibly opening the blades to facilitate the maneuver.
The surgeon places a blunt trocar into the morcellator port, preparing for tissue removal during the myomectomy procedure.

The surgeon attempts to grasp the tissue using a tenaculum, achieving a firm hold that avoids contact with the blade. The tenaculum provides a secure grip, facilitating the cutting process in cut mode.
The surgeon uses a new morselator blade to efficiently cut through the fibroid tissue, demonstrating the effectiveness of the sharp blade in the surgical field.
The surgeon demonstrates an alternative method for removing fibroids by slicing them, similar to the bisection technique used on the uterus.
The surgeon uses two tooth needle holders to securely grasp and bisect the fibroid, demonstrating the technique for effective tissue manipulation.
After bisecting the fibroid, the surgeon considers two removal options: extracting it through a ten mm trocar or performing a colpotomy from the posterior pouch for removal from below.
